contract: Brain reference/QA-Visual-Design-Gate.md § [S]-tier protocol — items map 1:1 to [J] judgment items; an [S] verdict pre-screens the human gate and never closes a [J] item.

verdict_schema.evidence: one sentence citing what is visible in the screenshot

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'CTA visible above fold (hero section)'
items.question: Is a primary call-to-action button clearly visible in the first screen-height of the page?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'CTA repeated every 2-3 scroll-depths'
items.question: Scanning the full page top to bottom, does a call-to-action reappear at least once every 2-3 screen-heights, with no long CTA-free stretch?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Social proof within 1 scroll of hero'
items.question: Within roughly one screen-height below the hero, is there visible social proof (testimonial, client result, named metric, or recognizable credibility marker)?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Mobile CTA thumb-reachable (bottom 60% of viewport)'
items.question: On this mobile capture, are primary CTAs positioned where a thumb can comfortably reach them (not exclusively pinned at the very top of each screen-height)?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'No dead-end pages (every page has a clear next action)'
items.question: Does the page end with a clear next action (CTA, booking module, or navigation pathway) rather than petering out into footer links only?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Guarantee statement adjacent to CTAs'
items.question: Is guarantee or risk-reversal language visible adjacent to at least one primary CTA?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Anti-slop compliance'
items.question: Does the page avoid generic AI-template aesthetics (stock gradient blobs, emoji bullets, uniform rounded cards, centered-everything monotony) in favor of a deliberate, specific design system?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Visual styling: site has its own identity'
items.question: Does the page read as a distinct, intentional brand identity (consistent palette, typography hierarchy, recurring geometric vocabulary) rather than an interchangeable template?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'No single-word lines on mobile headings'
items.question: Are all headings free of awkward single-word orphan lines at this mobile width?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Sticky nav does not overlap page content at ANY scroll position'
items.question: Is the navigation bar free of collisions with page content (no text or controls visually colliding with or hidden behind the nav)?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Hero headline has sufficient clearance from nav'
items.question: Does the hero headline have comfortable vertical clearance from the navigation (no cramped or colliding text on initial load)?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Testimonials have real attribution (first name + last initial minimum)'
items.question: If testimonials or quotes are visible, does each carry a real-looking attribution (name plus business/context), with no anonymous or generic 'Business Owner' quotes?

items.source: QA-Visual-Design-Gate.md Gate 2 'Testimonials: Not plain text'
items.question: If testimonials are visible, do they have deliberate visual treatment (quote styling, result badges, card structure) rather than plain paragraphs?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Entity clarity in first 200 words'
items.question: From the visible top-of-page copy, is it immediately clear who this business is, what it does, and for whom?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Content structured for retrieval (discrete FAQ pairs, summary blocks, clear sections)'
items.question: Is the page content organized into clearly delineated, scannable sections (headed blocks, FAQ pairs, summary modules) rather than undifferentiated prose?

items.source: QA-Visual-Design-Gate.md Gate 2 'No text walls — no section >120 words without a visual break'
items.question: Is every section free of text walls — no stretch of more than roughly 120 words of continuous body text without a visual break (card, chart, image, table, callout)?

items.source: QA-Visual-Design-Gate.md Gate 2 'Section Rhythm: no identical adjacent sections + background alternation'
items.question: Do adjacent sections vary in visual structure and background treatment (no two consecutive sections with identical layout that blur together)?

items.source: QA-Visual-Design-Gate.md Gate 2 'Hero: right-side visual exists'
items.question: Does the hero combine text with a substantive visual element (diagram, data visualization, product surface, or imagery) rather than text floating alone?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'No unverified dollar claims' (screenshot-visible attribution variant)
items.question: Does every specific dollar figure visible on the page carry visible attribution or source context (client result, industry average label, calculation basis)?

items.source: qa-checklist.md [J] 'Stats/metrics match BO proof points with source citation' (screenshot-visible variant)
items.question: Do displayed stats and metrics carry visible source or context cues rather than floating as bare unexplained numbers?

items.source: QA-Visual-Design-Gate.md Gate 2 'Logo: nav logo visible and appropriately sized'
items.question: Is the brand mark/logo clearly visible and appropriately sized in the navigation?
